title: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Koyfin (4.2★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Koyfin — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Koyfin?

Koyfin is a Bloomberg-style financial data terminal built for retail traders and investors who want institutional-quality data without the $24,000/year Bloomberg price tag. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in New York, it provides deep charting, fundamental analysis, macroeconomic data, and portfolio analytics across stocks, ETFs, forex, crypto, and fixed-income instruments globally. With a 4.2/5 rating and a solid free tier, Koyfin is best suited for fundamental analysts, macro investors, and financial advisors who prioritize data depth over real-time trade execution.

How to Create Your Koyfin Account

Step 1: Visit Koyfin and Sign Up

Go to koyfin.com and click the "Sign Up" button in the top right. You'll need to choose between a personal account or a professional/advisor account—select based on your trading style.

Step 2: Enter Your Email and Password

Provide your email address and create a strong password. You'll receive a verification email—click the link to confirm your address. This typically takes less than one minute.

Step 3: Choose Your Plan

Select from Free ($0/mo), Plus ($25/mo), Pro ($55/mo), or Premier ($119/mo). You can start with Free and upgrade anytime, so there's no pressure to commit immediately.

Step 4: Complete Your Profile (Optional for Free Tier)

If upgrading to a paid plan, Koyfin may ask for billing information and a few profile details. No advanced verification is required for retail accounts—no KYC, no ID upload. Setup time: 2–5 minutes total.

Setting Up Koyfin for the First Time

Dashboard Layout

When you first log in, you'll see a customizable dashboard with a sidebar for navigation and a main workspace. The left sidebar houses your watchlists, alerts, and saved screeners. Don't panic if it looks cluttered—you can delete or hide default items immediately.

Create Your First Watchlist

Click "Watchlists" in the sidebar and create a new list. Name it something like "Tech Growth Stocks" or "Dividend Portfolio." Add tickers by searching at the top. Koyfin will auto-populate price, fundamentals, and performance data for each holding. You can add unlimited tickers to a watchlist at no cost.

Configure Your Dashboard Widgets

Click the three-dot menu on any dashboard card to customize it. You can pin specific sectors, add macro indicators (GDP, unemployment, yields), or display your portfolio allocation. Rearrange widgets by dragging them. Save your layout—Koyfin remembers your preferences across sessions.

Set Your Notification Preferences

Go to Settings → Notifications and choose how you want to receive alerts: email, in-app, or mobile push. The free tier receives fewer notification slots than paid plans, but the default setup is conservative enough that you won't be overwhelmed.

Link Your Portfolio (Optional)

Koyfin's portfolio analytics feature lets you upload your holdings to track performance. You can manually enter positions or use a CSV import. This is optional and helps Koyfin personalize your dashboard, but it doesn't connect to your broker—it's a data entry tool only.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Advanced Charting with Fundamental Overlays

Koyfin's charting isn't as deep as TradingView, but its unique strength is overlaying fundamental data onto price charts. You can plot earnings surprises, dividend announcements, or insider trades directly on the candlestick chart. This is powerful for value investors—you see the fundamental catalyst and the market reaction in one view. Use this by opening any stock, clicking "Overlays," and selecting metrics like "EPS Surprise" or "Earnings Date."

2. Alerts System

Set price-based alerts (stock hits $150), or custom alerts for events like earnings announcements, dividend ex-dates, or analyst rating changes. Free tier gets limited alerts; Plus tier ($25/mo) unlocks more. Create an alert by clicking the bell icon on any stock and choosing your trigger condition. You'll receive notifications via your chosen channels.

3. Performance Analytics Dashboard

Portfolio analytics are Koyfin's bread and butter. Track your returns, analyze sector allocation, and compare performance against benchmarks. The dashboard breaks down your P&L by position, time period, and sector. If you're managing money or tracking multiple strategies, this is a key differentiator—most free charting tools don't offer this depth.

4. Macro Data and Economic Indicators

Koyfin integrates global macro data: Fed rates, unemployment, GDP, inflation, central bank policies. Create a custom dashboard tab for macro monitoring. This is essential for macro traders and anyone betting on currency or commodity moves. Click "Macro" in the sidebar to access 100+ global economic series.

5. News Feed Integration

A personalized news feed pulls stories from major outlets and aggregates them by your watchlist. You can read full articles in the Koyfin interface without leaving the platform. Paid tiers get faster news delivery and custom filters. This saves time versus bouncing between Bloomberg, Reuters, and Twitter.

6. Mobile App

Koyfin's iOS and Android apps sync your watchlists and alerts. The mobile app is functional but significantly less capable than the web platform—charting is simplified, and some advanced features aren't available. Use it for monitoring and alert management, not for deep analysis on the go.

7. Multi-Asset Screening

Screen across stocks, ETFs, crypto, and forex simultaneously. Build custom screeners using 200+ criteria: P/E ratios, insider buying, dividend yield, technical indicators. Save screeners and run them daily. Pro and Premier tiers get more screening slots and faster updates.

Koyfin Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free Tier ($0/mo)

You get watchlists, basic charting, limited alerts (5–10 active), macro data access, and a mobile app. Best for: beginner traders, investors who want to dip their toes in before paying, and those monitoring only a small portfolio. The free tier is genuinely useful—Koyfin doesn't artificially cripple it.

Plus Plan ($25/mo)

Unlocks more alerts (50+), expanded portfolio analytics, faster data updates, and ad-free experience. Best for: intermediate traders managing a single portfolio and want all the essentials without overkill. This is the sweet spot for cost-conscious fundamental investors.

Pro Plan ($55/mo)

Advanced charting, deeper historical data access, portfolio reporting tools, and API access for automating workflows. Best for: financial advisors, traders managing multiple portfolios, and anyone building custom analytics. The API is a game-changer if you want to pipe Koyfin data into your own spreadsheets or tools.

Premier Plan ($119/mo)

Everything in Pro, plus priority support, white-label options, and institutional-grade data (if needed). Best for: professional money managers, hedge funds, and financial institutions. Overkill for solo traders, but if you're managing client assets, the reporting and compliance features justify the cost.

Recommendation

Start free, upgrade to Plus ($25/mo) once you're using it daily. Only jump to Pro ($55/mo) if you're using Koyfin as your primary analytics platform or building workflows that feed other tools. Avoid Premier unless you manage outside capital.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Koyfin

1. Use Fundamental Overlays to Spot Earnings-Driven Rallies

Plot "Surprise %" on your chart alongside price. When earnings surprise overlaps with a price breakout, you're seeing the market repricing the stock in real time. Filter for consistent upside surprises—they often lead to follow-through moves. Zoom into quarterly earnings dates and identify your edge.

2. Build a Macro Dashboard for Your Market Thesis

If you trade oil, build a dashboard with Brent crude, USD index, Fed rate expectations, and global supply disruptions. Pin it in Koyfin and check it every morning. Your trade thesis needs context—Koyfin makes this easy in one dashboard instead of flipping between 5 tabs.

3. Create Screeners for Insider Buying + Technicals Confluence

Run a screener that finds stocks with insider buying in the last 30 days AND above their 50-day moving average. These are stocks with both insider conviction AND positive momentum. Insiders have time horizons measured in years, not days—respect their signal. Save this screener and run it weekly.

4. Use the News Feed to Identify Catalyst Timing

Koyfin aggregates earnings dates, FDA approvals, and deal announcements. Create a calendar view of upcoming events for your watchlist. You don't want to be caught off-guard when your position gaps on earnings—Koyfin helps you plan around these dates.

5. Link to TradingView for Precise Technical Entry Points

Use Koyfin for fundamentals, news, and macro context. Switch to TradingView when you need to nail entry/exit points with precise charting tools. This two-platform workflow is common among serious traders—Koyfin owns the "why," TradingView owns the "when."

6. Export Portfolio Performance Reports (Pro Tier and Up)

Generate PDF reports of your performance broken down by sector, time period, and holding. If you're managing money for others or want accountability, these reports are professional and detailed. Run them quarterly and track your progress.

7. Monitor Insider Trading Activity Before Public Earnings

Insiders often sell before bad news and buy before good news (sometimes months early). Watch the insider activity feed for large purchases at low prices—this is signaling future confidence. Pair this with analyst upgrades and you're spotting conviction plays.

Common Koyfin Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Alerts Aren't Triggering

Check that notifications are enabled in Settings → Notifications and your email isn't filtering Koyfin into spam. Also verify you're on the correct plan—some alert types require Plus or higher. Free tier alerts have daily limits; if you've hit your quota, they won't fire until tomorrow.

Issue 2: Historical Data Gaps or Slow Loading

Koyfin's data is solid, but international stocks and newer cryptos can have incomplete history. If a chart loads slowly, refresh the page and check your internet connection. Free and Plus tier users get slightly delayed data updates (15-minute delay)—upgrade to Pro for real-time if you need tick-level precision.

Issue 3: Mobile App Crashes or Syncing Issues

Log out of the mobile app and back in to re-sync. Clear your app cache (Settings → Apps → Koyfin → Storage → Clear Cache). If the app is still buggy, use the web version on mobile—Koyfin's web platform is mobile-responsive and more stable than the native app.

Issue 4: Too Many Notifications Cluttering Your Inbox

Go to Settings and disable alerts you don't need. Create a separate email filter for Koyfin notifications to keep them out of your main inbox. Be aggressive about pruning—you only need alerts for your core positions and key macro levels, not every minor price movement.

Is Koyfin Worth It? Our Verdict

Koyfin deserves its 4.2/5 rating. It's an exceptional value for fundamental analysts and macro investors—you get Bloomberg-quality data at 1/100th the cost. The charting trails TradingView, and there's no trade execution, but that's by design. Choose Koyfin if you're a long-term investor, financial advisor, or value trader who wants deep fundamentals, macro context, and portfolio analytics. Skip it if you're a day trader needing microsecond execution or a technical analyst requiring advanced charting (use TradingView or thinkorswim instead). For most retail investors managing a buy-and-hold portfolio, the Plus tier ($25/mo) is the best-in-class analytical tool on the market today.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
